twitter_thread: 63% of households can't afford a $1k emergency. That's 77 million households in the US alone.
I sold $1,200 worth of unused items on eBay, Craigslist, and Facebook Marketplace in one month
Renting out a spare room on Airbnb can generate $1,050/mo in cities like San Francisco, but beware of 15% host fees
Upwork's top freelance writers earn $60/hr, but 75% of applicants are rejected due to low-quality portfolios
VIPKid pays $22/hr to teach English online, with a 6-hour scheduling minimum and $100 bonus for referrals
Honey's AI-powered cashback tool earned me $210 last year on routine Amazon purchases
What's the most unexpected way you've made money to fill a financial gap? #affordabilitycrisis #financialresilience
pack.linkedin: 63% of households can't afford a $1k emergency, affecting 77 million households in the US. Selling unused items on eBay, Craigslist, or Facebook Marketplace can net $1,200/mo, as I've done personally. Renting out a spare room on Airbnb can generate $1,050/mo in cities like San Francisco, but beware of 15% host fees. VIPKid pays $22/hr to teach English online, with a 6-hour scheduling minimum and $100 bonus for referrals. Using AI-powered tools like Honey for cashback and rewards can add up to $210/yr.
